name: CAM Expansion Velocity as Distress Precursor
mechanism: When auditors add Critical Audit Matters in new topic clusters not previously associated with a company, it signals escalating accounting complexity that precedes visible financial deterioration by 1-3 quarters. LLM advantage: systematic CAM topic clustering across the entire market — embedding CAM paragraphs, building a market-wide taxonomy, and detecting when a company's CAM set expands into new clusters.
llm_advantage: CAMs are mandatory PCAOB AS 3101 disclosures that no existing commercial data product systematically parses. The LLM embeds CAM paragraphs across all public companies, builds a market-wide CAM topic taxonomy via clustering, and detects CAM expansion into new topic clusters — a cross-sectional anomaly detection no existing system performs.
why_underweighted: CAMs are new (mandatory since mid-2019), the academic literature is thin (focused on audit fees not stock returns), and the market views the auditor's report as a binary pass/fail signal rather than a continuous multi-dimensional signal source. The structural lag (auditors identify problems before they appear in financials) means the information is not immediately priced.

known_biases: CAMs only mandatory since mid-2019 — limited historical data for long-horizon backtests
CAM language varies by audit firm (Deloitte vs PwC vs EY vs KPMG) requiring robust cross-firm embeddings
Most companies have stable CAM sets year-over-year — signal fires infrequently
Structural reporting lag: CAMs filed 45-90 days after fiscal year-end means 1-2 earnings releases have already occurred
Survivorship bias for stocks delisted before ~2017

falsifiable_prediction: Companies with CAM expansion into new topic clusters should underperform companies with stable CAM sets by at least 500bps annualized over the subsequent 6 months. The effect should persist even when recent earnings have been stable/growing, confirming auditors identify latent problems not yet visible in reported financials.
biggest_weakness: Auditor timeliness: even with the stable-earnings test, the structural lag means CAM expansion must provide incremental information beyond what the market can already observe from quarterly earnings releases. Also, 5-6 years of data is limited for a 6-month holding period.
